notes: Security: Helmet + CORS applied, path sanitization with regex, UUID validation on X-Request-Id, no secrets leaked in responses, body size limit. Deduction: CORS is fully open (no origin whitelist), /metrics and /cache endpoints have no authentication (only rate limiting), DELETE /cache/:key reflects user-supplied key in response (minor reflected content risk). Error handling: comprehensive global error handler distinguishing parse errors/entity too large/SyntaxError/URIError, timeout detection via AbortError, upstream 401 vs 5xx differentiated, all error paths return requestId. Deduction: upstream.json() can throw if body is malformed despite content-type header claiming JSON (no try/catch around that specific call). Best practices: good SoC with distinct classes (KeyRotator, CircuitBreaker, TTLCache, SlidingWindowRateLimiter), configurable via env, metrics collector, clear section headers. Deductions: shutdown middleware (line 794) is registered AFTER routes so it will never execute (Express evaluates middleware in order), isValidUUID and sanitizePath are defined but UUID_RE is only used once, routeHandlers extensibility pattern is over-engineered for 2 entries. The sliding window uses shift() which is O(n) — acceptable for small windows but not ideal. Robustness: request timeout (30s AbortSignal), graceful shutdown with connection tracking and 10s force-kill, periodic cache sweep, rate limiter cleanup, deduplication via idempotency keys. Deductions: no max cache size (unbounded memory growth possible), no per-IP connection limit beyond rate limiting, HALF_OPEN state allows unlimited concurrent probes (should limit to 1), sliding window timestamps array has no cap.
